title: How to Use a VPN for Streaming: The Complete 2026 Guide
description: Everything you need to know about using a VPN for streaming in 2026. Which VPNs work, which platforms they unblock, and how to set it up on any device.
content:
## How to Use a VPN for Streaming

Streaming services lock their content libraries by country. Netflix US has thousands of titles not available on Netflix UK. BBC iPlayer is only available in the UK. Hulu is US-only. A VPN changes your apparent location, unlocking whichever library you want.

This guide covers everything: which VPNs work, which platforms they unblock, how to set them up, and what to do when a streaming site blocks your VPN.

## How VPN Streaming Actually Works

When you connect to a VPN server in the US, your device appears to be in the US. Netflix, Hulu, and other streaming services check your IP address to determine your location. The VPN server's IP address is what they see — not your real one.

Streaming platforms know about VPNs and actively try to block them. They maintain blocklists of known VPN IP addresses. Good VPN providers rotate their IP addresses frequently to stay ahead of these blocklists.

## Which VPNs Work for Streaming?

Not all VPNs unblock streaming reliably. The ones that do:

**NordVPN** — Most consistent for Netflix, works with BBC iPlayer, Hulu, Disney+, Amazon Prime, and most other services. Large server pool means more IP rotation when platforms update blocklists.

**ExpressVPN** — Premium choice, works in more countries and with more platforms than most. Reliable even when Netflix pushes aggressive IP blocking updates.

**Surfshark** — Good streaming performance, unlimited devices means you can use it on your TV, laptop, and phone simultaneously.

**CyberGhost** — Has servers specifically labeled for Netflix, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, and other platforms. Less guesswork about which server to use.

**NordVPN is our top pick for streaming** — largest server pool, consistent unblocking, and strong speeds for buffer-free 4K.

## Platform by Platform: What Works

**Netflix**
Netflix is the most VPN-aware streaming service. Connect to a US server for the largest library. UK, Japan, and Canada servers unlock regional exclusives. The above four VPNs all work reliably.

**BBC iPlayer**
Requires a UK IP address. Also requires a free BBC account. NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark work consistently. Note: BBC iPlayer requires you to confirm you have a UK TV licence — this is on the honour system.

**Hulu**
US-only service. Connect to any US server. Also works: Disney+, Peacock, Paramount+, and most US streaming services.

**Disney+**
Works with most VPNs. Regional libraries vary — Japanese Disney+ and US Disney+ have different catalogue.

**Amazon Prime Video**
Generally easier to unblock than Netflix. Most VPNs work. Different regional libraries available.

**DAZN**
One of the harder platforms to unblock. NordVPN and ExpressVPN work most reliably.

## Step-by-Step Setup

**On a computer (Windows/Mac):**
1. Sign up for your VPN (NordVPN recommended)
2. Download and install the app
3. Open the app and connect to a server in your target country
4. Open your browser and go to the streaming service
5. If prompted to log in, use your existing account

**On iPhone/iPad:**
1. Download the VPN app from the App Store
2. Allow VPN permissions when prompted
3. Connect to your target server
4. Open the streaming app normally

**On Android:**
1. Download from Google Play Store
2. Same steps as iPhone

**On Firestick/Fire TV:**
1. Search for your VPN in the Amazon App Store (IPVanish and NordVPN have official Fire TV apps)
2. Install, log in, and connect
3. Open your streaming app — no browser needed

**On Smart TV (Samsung/LG):**
Most smart TVs can't run VPN apps directly. Two options:
- Set up the VPN on your router (protects all devices automatically)
- Share a VPN connection from your laptop via Wi-Fi hotspot

**On a router:**
ExpressVPN, NordVPN, and Surfshark all have router setup guides. Once installed on your router, every device at home — including smart TVs, consoles, and streaming sticks — gets the VPN automatically.

## What to Do When a Streaming Site Blocks You

If you see an error like "You seem to be using an unblocker or proxy" on Netflix:

1. **Try a different server** in the same country — some servers get blocked, others don't
2. **Clear your browser cookies** — old location data can persist
3. **Use a different browser** or try the streaming app instead of the website
4. **Contact VPN support** — good VPNs monitor streaming compatibility and will tell you which specific server to use

## Speed Requirements for Streaming

You need enough speed through your VPN connection:
- Standard definition (SD): 3 Mbps
- High definition (HD, 1080p): 5 Mbps
- 4K Ultra HD: 25 Mbps

A good paid VPN on WireGuard will easily provide these speeds on nearby servers. If you're streaming 4K and experiencing buffering, try a server in a closer location or switch to WireGuard protocol.

## Streaming and VPN Legality

Using a VPN for streaming is generally legal. Most streaming service terms of service prohibit bypassing geo-restrictions, which means you're violating terms of service — not law. The worst realistic consequence is having your account suspended.

In practice, streaming services rarely suspend accounts for VPN use. They prefer to block VPN IPs rather than punish individual subscribers.
